figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic of the velocity-block sweeping strategy. The discrete velocity space is divided into blocks of size Bv. Each block is used as the basic unit for microscopic reconstruction, nonequilibrium flux evaluation, moment accumulation, and the first-stage microscopic update.

Figure 2: Triple-buffered velocity-block pipeline. During the sweep of velocity block m, the current gradient buffer is consumed by nonequilibrium flux evaluation, another gradient buffer is associated with MPI halo exchange for block m + 1, and the third buffer is written by the reconstruction of block m + 2. The communication package contains {fm+3,∇fm+1}.

Figure 3: Schematic of the two-stream execution model. The computation stream evaluates reconstruction, nonequilibrium fluxes, local reductions, and microscopic updates. The communication stream packs halo data, performs non-blocking MPI communication, and unpacks received data. Device events are used only at necessary dependency points, allowing part of the next-block communication to overlap with the computation of the current block.

Figure 4: Block-level data reuse and local moment reduction. For one physical interface, the geometric and macroscopic interface data are shared by all velocity threads in the block. Each thread computes one velocity-point contribution, and the partial macroscopic moment is reduced inside the thread group before a block-level contribution is written to global memory.
Figure 5: Centerline velocity profiles for the three-dimensional lid-driven cavity flow. Symbols denote DVM reference data, and solid lines denote the FPP-UGKS results.
Figure 6: Temperature distributions of the three-dimensional lid-driven cavity flow.

Figure 7: Strong and weak scalability for Bv = 32. Panels show (a) strong-scaling speedup, (b) strong-scaling parallel efficiency, (c) workload-weighted weak-scaling efficiency, and (d) time per cell in the strong-scaling test.

Figure 8: Strong and weak scalability for Bv = 64. Panels show (a) strong-scaling speedup, (b) strong-scaling parallel efficiency, (c) workload-weighted weak-scaling efficiency, and (d) time per cell in the strong-scaling test.

Figure 9: Strong and weak scalability for Bv = 128. Panels show (a) strong-scaling speedup, (b) strongscaling parallel efficiency, (c) workload-weighted weak-scaling efficiency, and (d) time per cell in the strongscaling test.

Figure 10: Strong and weak scalability for Bv = 256. Panels show (a) strong-scaling speedup, (b) strongscaling parallel efficiency, (c) workload-weighted weak-scaling efficiency, and (d) time per cell in the strongscaling test.
Figure 11: Physical-space and velocity-space meshes for the supersonic sphere-flow calculation.
Figure 12: Mach-number distributions for supersonic flow around a sphere at M∞ = 4.25.
Figure 13: Temperature distributions for supersonic flow around a sphere at M∞ = 4.25.
Figure 14: Pressure coefficient Cp on the sphere surface. Symbols denote DSMC reference data from Ref. [51], and solid lines denote the present FPP-UGKS results.
Figure 15: Heat-transfer coefficient Ch on the sphere surface. Symbols denote DSMC reference data from Ref. [51], and solid lines denote the present FPP-UGKS results.
Figure 16: Tangential-stress coefficient Ct on the sphere surface. Symbols denote DSMC reference data from Ref. [51], and solid lines denote the present FPP-UGKS results.
Figure 17: Physical-space and velocity-space meshes for the X38-like vehicle calculation.
Figure 18: Computed temperature, Mach-number, and pressure distributions around the X38-like vehicle at M∞ = 8.0, α = 20◦, and Kn = 0.00275.
Figure 19: Surface coefficient distributions on the X38-like vehicle. Symbols denote DSMC reference data from Ref. [53], and solid lines denote the present FPP-UGKS results.
Figure 20: Physical-space and surface meshes for the Orion-like capsule calculation.
Figure 21: Computed temperature, pressure, Mach-number, and streamline distributions around the Orionlike capsule at M∞ = 8.0 and Kn = 0.00275.

Figure B.1 Representative NVIDIA Nsight Systems timeline for the X38-like vehicle calculation, illustrating the repeated velocity-block execution pattern and the interaction between GPU kernels, memory transfers, MPI waiting intervals, and CUDA stream synchronization.
